⛮
Platform Workflow Docs GitHub Start

Self-hosted agent runtime

The agent that runs anywhere.

Arbiter is one small binary for laptops, servers, CI runners, edge boxes, and the systems around them. Use the TUI, one-shot CLI, HTTP API, or event router to turn real work into supervised agent runs.

Install Arbiter curl -fsSL https://arbiter.run/install.sh | sh
RouteMap prompts, webhooks, incidents, schedules, and hardware events to the right agent.
SuperviseConstrain tools and require advisor review before consequential work leaves the runtime.
ReplayPersist request streams so clients can reconnect, audit, or tail in-flight work.

The control plane for agentic operations.

Coders need IDE agents. People need chat assistants. Systems need a runtime that can receive events, choose an agent, execute tools under policy, and leave an auditable trail.

For operators

Route production incidents, scheduled checks, queue messages, and service webhooks into supervised agent runs.

For developers

Drive the same agents from the TUI, one-shot CLI, HTTP API, or Agent2Agent clients without changing the runtime.

For devices

Let sensors and edge machines emit structured events while Arbiter handles routing, memory, tools, and final action.

How it works

One event stream from request to result.

Arbiter makes the invisible parts of agent work visible: routing, delegation, tool calls, memory reads, advisor gates, artifacts, token use, and terminal status.

1Receive

Accept a prompt, CLI send, HTTP request, webhook, schedule, or device event.

2Route

Match the work to an agent constitution with explicit tools, memory, and event patterns.

3Act

Execute writ commands inline while advisor gates review consequential decisions.

4Observe

Stream and persist every event so clients can reconnect, inspect, and audit outcomes.

Built for the space between copilots and cron jobs.

Arbiter is not trying to be your editor, your hosted assistant, or a chat app. It is the runtime layer underneath agentic workflows that need local control and operational visibility.

Self-hosted by default

Your binary, your machine, your tenant database, your agent constitutions, and your provider keys.

Policy in the loop

Tool access is an allowlist. Advisor gates are structural checks, not another prompt asking the same model to self-grade.

Interfaces are optional

Use the terminal, script it, embed the API, federate over A2A, or let events arrive from systems that never show a UI.

Documentation that ships with the code.

This site ingests the repository Markdown directly, preserving the docs authoring flow while publishing static HTML.

getting-startedInstall locally, configure providers, and get the first agent reply. philosophyThe design themes behind Arbiter's runtime, memory, and supervision model. apiHTTP, SSE, tenants, requests, conversations, schedules, agents, and A2A endpoints.
Arbiter is experimental open-source infrastructure. Philosophy
